market_session: US weekend no-session; sentiment values are latest available pre-weekend source readings
text: The June 20, 2026 report date was a Saturday no-session day for US cash equities, immediately after the NYSE-listed Juneteenth National Independence Day closure on Friday, June 19. Sentiment readings in this cycle are therefore latest-available pre-weekend source readings, not a Saturday trading print.

subject.name: CNN Fear & Greed Index
subject.source_basis: public CNN endpoint mirror corroborated by MacroMicro

value.direct_cnn_fetch_status: blocked by endpoint bot response during collection

market_session: latest available pre-weekend CNN Fear & Greed mirror reading
text: The CNN Fear & Greed mirror showed a June 19 score of 37.26, rated fear, slightly above the prior-week value of 35.51 but far below the prior-month value of 59.37. MacroMicro corroborated the June 19 level at 37.26. CNN's live endpoint blocked automated fetches during this cycle, so the claim is based on the documented public mirror and MacroMicro corroboration, not a claimed direct CNN retrieval.

market_session: Cboe published volatility-index CSV rows; latest common curve date 2026-06-18

calculation.method: VIX one-day percent change = (16.78 - 16.40) / 16.40 * 100 = 2.3171%; latest common curve compares Cboe VIX9D, VIX, VIX3M, and VIX6M close fields on 2026-06-18

text: Cboe's VIX CSV showed VIX at 16.78 on its June 19 row, up 0.38 points or about 2.32% from June 18. On the latest common curve date, June 18, VIX9D closed at 13.93, VIX at 16.40, VIX3M at 19.57, and VIX6M at 21.99, an upward-sloping curve consistent with contained spot stress rather than front-end panic.

market_session: latest dated Cboe-sourced YCharts period, corroborated by Cboe daily statistics page accessed 2026-06-20
text: Cboe-sourced YCharts pages listed June 18 readings of 0.91 for total put/call and 0.58 for equity put/call, and Cboe's daily market-statistics page showed the same current ratios. The total ratio points to aggregate hedging demand, while the equity-only ratio below 0.6 shows single-stock options flow remained call-heavy.

market_session: weekly AAII sentiment survey published before the report-date weekend

calculation.method: Bull-bear spread = bullish 36.6% minus bearish 39.4% = -2.8 percentage points

text: AAII's June 18 sentiment post showed bullish sentiment up to 36.6%, neutral sentiment up to 24.1%, and bearish sentiment down to 39.4%, leaving the bull-bear spread at about -2.8 percentage points. Retail sentiment was still net bearish, but materially less pessimistic than the prior survey.

market_session: latest available fund-flow releases before the report-date weekend
text: Fund-flow data were more constructive than the survey and composite gauges: ICI reported $31.52 billion of long-term fund and ETF inflows for the week ended June 10, including $48.33 billion of ETF net issuance, while ETF.com reported $41.80 billion of US-listed ETF inflows for the week ended June 12 and $64.55 billion of daily ETF inflows on its June 18 screen. US equity and semiconductor flows were strong, though commodity ETFs and some large proxies such as QQQ and GLD had earlier weekly redemptions.

value.volatility: contained spot stress with upward-sloping curve
value.flows: strong equity and semiconductor allocations into the weekend

market_session: weekend synthesis from latest available pre-weekend sentiment indicators
text: The best synthesis for June 20 is 'fear headline, risk-appetite flows': Fear & Greed and AAII remained defensive, volatility was contained rather than panicked, put/call ratios showed aggregate hedging but call-heavy equity options, and ETF flows still chased US equities and semiconductors. The setup is fragile but not outright bearish into the June 22 reopen.
